channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Nonprofit groups and Democrats sue Trump administration over election executive order
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — President Donald Trump's executive order seeking to overhaul the nation's elections faced its first legal challenges Monday as the Democratic National Committee and a pair of nonprofits filed two separate lawsuits calling it unconstitutional.
item.dc:creator: ALI SWENSON, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Wild weather blamed for deaths of 3 kids in Michigan and a man in an Amish buggy in Indiana
item.description: KALAMAZOO, Mich. (AP) — A tree fell on a van in Michigan, killing three children, during a fierce weekend storm that swept the region and contributed to the death of an 84-year-old man in an Amish buggy in Indiana, authorities said Monday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Judge pauses Trump administration plans to end temporary legal protections for Venezuelans
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — A federal judge on Monday paused plans by the Trump administration to end temporary legal protections for hundreds of thousands of Venezuelans, a week before they were scheduled to expire. The order by U.S.
item.dc:creator: JANIE HAR, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Luna accuses House Freedom Caucus of smear; exits group over parental proxy voting battle
item.description: Rep. Anna Paulina Luna (R-Fla.) announced Monday that she is leaving the House Freedom Caucus amid her battle with GOP leadership and hard-line Republicans over her push to allow proxy voting for new parents.
item.dc:creator: Mychael Schnell, WFLA, NBC

item.title: 20 staffers accused of abusing students or not reporting it at Pennsylvania charter school
item.description: Twenty staffers at a suburban Philadelphia charter school are facing charges related to the alleged physical abuse of students using painful, unapproved techniques to restrain and punish youths in a program meant to help them deal with emotional issues, authorities announced Monday.
item.dc:creator: BRUCE SHIPKOWSKI, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump has dubbed April 2 'Liberation Day' for his tariffs. Here's what to expect
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — As the trade wars launched by U.S. President Donald Trump continue to escalate, all eyes are on Wednesday. Trump has repeatedly called April 2 “Liberation Day,” with promises to roll out a set of tariffs, or taxes on imports from other countries, that he says will free the U.S.
item.dc:creator: WYATTE GRANTHAM-PHILIPS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Report: 1 in 5 Catholics in US affected by deportations
item.description: One in five Catholic families in the United States stands to feel the pain from the Trump administration's promised mass deportations, a group of faith leaders said.
item.dc:creator: Julian Resendiz, Fox 5

item.title: Judge blocks release of some records related to deaths of Gene Hackman, Betsy Arakawa
item.description: A New Mexico judge has decided to block the release of public records showing the bodies of Gene Hackman and his wife Betsy Arakawa.
item.dc:creator: Isaac Cruz, WFLA, NBC

item.title: NASA astronauts Butch Wilmore, Suni Williams discuss extended ISS stay
item.description: NASA astronauts Butch Wilmore blasted into orbit in June 2024, as part of a test flight of Boeing’s new Starliner crew capsule. They were scheduled to return about eight days later, but problems plagued the mission.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, ABC 4

item.title: A lawsuit by inmates is challenging Tennessee's plans to resume executions. Here's how
item.description: NASHVILLE, Tenn. (AP) — Nine Tennessee death row inmates are suing the state over its push for a new round of lethal injections after an execution was abruptly called off in 2022 and a follow-up investigation found scores of missteps in several executions.
item.dc:creator: JONATHAN MATTISE, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Sewage concerns arise as county plans to reopen San Diego campground
item.description: Though the campground offers stunning views of the valley, enjoying all that it has to offer could be risky due to the ongoing sewage problem.
item.dc:creator: Amber Coakley, NewsNation

item.title: Epstein victim Virginia Giuffre says she has four days to live
item.description: Giuffre didn’t disclose when the accident happened, but said it has been the “worst start to the new year.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, WFLA, NBC

item.title: US auto companies can eat costs of tariffs on parts: Trump
item.description: President Trump's senior trade adviser, Peter Navarro, says the tariffs plan will raise more than $6 trillion in federal revenue over the next decade.
item.dc:creator: Kellie Meyer, NewsNation

item.title: 60 percent disapprove of Trump tariffs: Survey
item.description: Just 38 percent of respondents said they approve of Trump's approach to trade negotiations with other countries.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Crisp, NewsNation

item.title: Epstein victim Virginia Giuffre says she has four days to live
item.description: Giuffre didn’t disclose when the accident happened, but said it has been the “worst start to the new year.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, NewsNation

item.title: Sheriff: No way to prevent tree falling on minivan, killing kids
item.description: "Given the volume of the tree, the weight of the tree and where it struck, there is no vehicle that would stand up to this," said Sheriff Richard Fuller.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Van Gilder, NewsNation

item.title: Tennessee bill to requires schools to teach ‘success sequence’ of life path
item.description: The "Success Sequence Act" would require schools to teach the positive outcomes associated with completing the following in the following order: graduate high school, enter the workforce or go to college, get married, and then have children.
item.dc:creator: Tori Gessner, NewsNation

item.title: Trump is stronger on immigration and weaker on trade, an AP-NORC poll finds
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Immigration remains a strength for President Donald Trump, but his handling of tariffs is getting more negative feedback, according to a poll from The Associated Press-NORC Center for Public Affairs Research. About half of U.S.
item.dc:creator: AMELIA THOMSON-DEVEAUX and LINLEY SANDERS, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Harris pleaded with Biden for speedy endorsement
item.description: Former Vice President Kamala Harris pleaded with former President Biden to endorse her White House bid on the same day as his historic move to end his 2024 reelection bid, according to exclusive excerpts from a forthcoming book.
item.dc:creator: Julia Mueller, NewsNation

item.title: Driver facing multiple charges in Brooklyn crash that killed mother, two children
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — A woman is facing multiple charges after she crashed into a family out for a walk in Brooklyn, killing a mother and her two children in what New York City Mayor Eric Adams described as a “tragic accident of a Shakespearean proportion.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Protests against Elon Musk's purge of US government swarm Tesla showrooms
item.description: Protesters against billionaire Elon Musk’s purge of the U.S. government under President Donald Trump demonstrated outside Tesla dealerships throughout the U.S. and in some cities in Europe on Saturday in the latest attempt to dent the fortune of the world’s richest man.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL LIEDTKE, Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: 3 US soldiers found dead in armored vehicle in Lithuania; 1 still missing
item.description: Three of the U.S. Army soldiers who went missing in Lithuania have been found dead in their armored vehicle that was pulled from a swampy area early Monday, according to U.S. Army Europe and Africa Command. Another soldier is still missing.
item.dc:creator: By LIUDAS DAPKUS and LOLITA C. BALDOR Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Cause of death determined for 3 young American women who died at Belize resort
item.description: A cause of death has been determined for three young women from the United States found dead in their hotel room at a resort in San Pedro, Belize, late last month.
item.dc:creator: Josh DuBose, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Judge blocks release of some records related to deaths of Gene Hackman, Betsy Arakawa
item.description: A New Mexico judge has decided to block the release of public records showing the bodies of Gene Hackman and his wife Betsy Arakawa.
item.dc:creator: Isaac Cruz, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Explosion at Detroit apartment building injures residents, including several children
item.description: When firefighters arrived, some people were in windows of the building “kind of hanging off ready to jump,” Fire Commissioner Chuck Simms told reporters.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Expelled DC councilmember teases run for old seat despite federal bribery charge
item.description: Trayon White, an expelled D.C. councilmember, is teasing a re-election campaign for his old Ward 8 seat despite facing a federal bribery charge.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Mortgage company Rocket buying Mr. Cooper in all-stock deal valued at $9.4 billion
item.description: The mortgage company Rocket is buying competitor Mr. Cooper in an all-stock deal valued at $9.4 billion, just weeks after acquiring real estate listing company Redfin.
item.dc:creator: MICHELLE CHAPMAN AP Business Writer, WFLA, NBC

item.title: A court is allowing the release of most records in the Gene Hackman death investigation
item.description: A New Mexico court has cleared the way for the release of investigative records from the deaths of Gene Hackman and his wife, Betsy Arakawa, as long as depictions of the deceased couple are blocked from view.
item.dc:creator: Morgan Lee, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Iran rejects direct negotiations with the US in response to Trump's letter
item.description: Iran's president said Sunday that the Islamic Republic rejected direct negotiations with the United States over its rapidly advancing nuclear program, offering Tehran's first response to a letter President Donald Trump sent to the country's supreme leader.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Lauren Betts and UCLA reach first Final Four of women's NCAA Tournament, beating LSU 72-65
item.description: SPOKANE, Wash. (AP) — Lauren Betts had 17 points and seven rebounds despite spending the entire second quarter on the bench, and top overall seed UCLA reached its first Final Four of the women's NCAA Tournament with a 72-65 victory over LSU on Sunday.
item.dc:creator: ANNE M. PETERSON, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Woman receives 100 cancer treatments, living far beyond doctors' expectations
item.description: An 84-year-old Utah woman recently received her 100th cancer treatment, outliving doctors' expectations.
item.dc:creator: Yeonseung Kim, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump roars down multiple paths of retribution as he vowed. Some targets yield while others fight
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The executive order directed at one of the country's most prestigious law firms followed a well-worn playbook as President Donald Trump roared down the road to retribution.
item.dc:creator: ERIC TUCKER and CALVIN WOODWARD, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: At least 4 dead after severe storms in the South Texas-Mexico border region and hundreds rescued
item.description: McALLEN, Texas (AP) — Drenching rains along the Texas-Mexico border trapped hundreds of people in flooded homes and in cars stranded in high waters, scrambling rescue crews to calls for help that continued Friday even as the downpours let up. At least four people died, including some who drowned.
item.dc:creator: VALERIE GONZALEZ and JUAN A. LOZANO, Associated Press, ABC 4
